question_title: Will the interest in “travel warning” change between 2026-04-14 and 2026-04-23 according to Google Trends?
summary: The "travel warning" search term spiked to 100 on Apr 13, 2026 (driven by US State Dept worldwide travel advisory expansion related to the Iran war), then dropped to 22 on Apr 14. The question compares the Apr 14 value (22) to the Apr 23 value in the resolution window. Post-spike decay is the dominant dynamic: historically, even smaller spikes (16) decayed to baseline (3-6) within weeks. Monte Carlo simulation with 50k runs, modeling decay with uncertain half-life (median 3 days), variable floor (1-10 depending on crisis persistence), and 15% re-spike probability, yields ~86% Decreases. Adjusted slightly upward for Increases (ongoing Iran war could produce new escalation) and Doesn't Change (SerpAPI measurement uncertainty). The narrow "Doesn't change" band (19-25) makes that outcome genuinely unlikely — most decay trajectories land well below 19, and most re-spikes would exceed 25.

factors.description: Massive spike on Apr 13 (100) already decaying rapidly to 22 on Apr 14 - post-spike decay strongly favors values well below 19 by Apr 23
Historical analogy: Feb spike of 16 decayed to 12→8→8→9→3 over ~5 weeks; current much larger spike should also decay substantially in 9 days
Ongoing Iran war and US worldwide travel advisory (issued Mar 22, expanded Apr 8-10) create active news cycle that could re-spike interest above 25
Multiple new travel advisories being issued in rapid succession (Azerbaijan, São Tomé, Nigeria, Albania) - ongoing stream of catalysts for renewed interest
Narrow 'Doesn't change' band (19-25) is only 7 integer values wide; most scenarios either decay well below or re-spike well above this range
SerpAPI vs pytrends measurement uncertainty of ±2 points adds noise to borderline outcomes near the ±3 threshold

reasoning:
**Forecast: Decreases (79%), Increases (13%), Doesn't Change (8%)**

**Key reasoning:**

- **Starting point:** Apr 14 = 22 in the resolution window (2026-03-24 to 2026-04-23), after a massive spike to 100 on Apr 13 driven by US State Department worldwide travel advisory expansion (Iran war).
- **Threshold math:** Increases requires Apr 23 > 25; Decreases requires < 19; Doesn't change covers 19-25.
- **Post-spike decay dominates:** The 100→22 single-day drop (78% decline) shows the initial curiosity wave already passed. Historical analogues show spikes in this term decay to baseline (3-6) within weeks. Monte Carlo simulation (50k runs) projects median Apr 23 value around 8.
- **Increases risk (~13%):** The Iran war is an active ongoing crisis, not a one-off event. New travel advisories are being issued regularly. A major new escalation could re-spike interest above 25.
- **Doesn't change is unlikely (~8%):** The 19-25 band is narrow. Most decay paths land well below 19; most re-spike scenarios overshoot above 25. Only measurement uncertainty and a very specific decay trajectory could land here.
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) Will the interest in “travel warning” change between 2026-04-14 and 2026-04-23 according to Google Trends?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/43210)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves “Doesn't change” if the value on the timeline at [https://trends.google.com/trends/explore?geo=US&tz=0&q=travel%20warning&date=2026-03-24%202026-04-23](https://trends.google.com/trends/explore?geo=US&tz=0&q=travel%20warning&date=2026-03-24%202026-04-23) for 2026-04-23 is within 3 of the value at 2026-04-14. It resolves “Increases” if the value is more than 3 greater, and resolves “Decreases” if the value is more than 3 lower.
fine_print:
A script will be used to determine the resolution of this question. It will access the data on Google Trends using [SerpApi](https://serpapi.com/), and compare the height of the timeline for the two aforementioned dates. The specific python query will be structured as follows:
```
from serpapi import GoogleSearch

search = GoogleSearch(params={
  "api_key": API_KEY,
  "engine": "google_trends",
  "data_type": "TIMESERIES",
  "geo": "US",
  "tz": 0,
  "q": "travel warning",
  "date": "2026-03-24 2026-04-23",
})
search.get_dict()
```
Note that there may be differences between the results returned by the API and the data appearing on the page. This seems to be due to the 'tz' parameter not having the intended effect in-browser. In this case, the API results will be considered authoritative.
Note that the precision of the timeline will be 1 day, so this will compare the overall interest for the whole day as determined by Google Trends.
Dates are determined in UTC.
If the data is no longer available, or the script fails, this question will be annulled or manually resolved by a moderator.
